meaning: n.好色之徒,感觉论者,感觉主义者
examples: Somewhere between this extreme repression of the monk and the license of the sensualist lies the truth.
I am not absolutely such a fool and sensualist as to regret the absence of a carpet, a sofa, and silver plate.
The middle notes of delicate and tender flowers somewhat soften this sensualist whirlwind leaving Wild Rose and Freesia to flourish.
Charles (Antoine Monnier), a quietly imperious sensualist of blazing intelligence, lives idly in a bare garret and does little but brazenly chase women.
He was a Rabelaisian sensualist, not a Balzacian crook.
